Vancouver Street Church destroyed in fire on Downtown Eastside, no major injuries   Flames broke out at about 9:30 p.m. Wednesday in a two-storey building in the 100 block of East Hastings Street and quickly spread to the adjoining two-storey structure housing the Vancouver Street Church.

COVID-19 update for July 7: Half of Canadians were infected with Omicron in only 5 months | Omicron has set stage for summer of disruption: Epidemiologist   Here's your daily update with everything you need to know on the coronavirus situation in B.C. and around the world.

President of TEAM Vancouver resigns, saying the party is not pro-development enough   Barj Dhahan told some members he was stepping down because he was not confident "that TEAM's ongoing opposition to development is sufficient to earn positive results on October 15."
Dan Fumano: Vancouver plan a break from the past, top planner says   The city's top planner says past plans and practices are insufficient for today's challenges

Elon Musk 'secretly' welcomed twins with top Neuralink exec   The new additions mean Musk, 51, now has nine children
CAUGHT ON CAMERA: Neighbourhood fireworks show goes spectacularly wrong   It is unknown if anyone was harmed, but RIP to the minivan.

Half of Canadians were infected with Omicron in only five months: report   'Omicron has been a tsunami,' with an average of 100,000 infections per day from December to May, says the COVID-19 Immunity Task Force

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

Home sales plummet in Metro Vancouver as buyers adjust to interest rate hikes   Prospective buyers are now being forced to weigh the positive impact of falling prices against the negative impact of rising interest rates

How one man founded Canada's craft beer industry right here in B.C.   To commemorate the 40th anniversary of the first craft ale at Horseshoe Bay's The Troller pub, John Ohler has teamed up with Russell Brewing to launch the John Mitchell Signature Cascadian Dark Ale.
CRA completes 61,000 audits of Toronto, Vancouver property owners   CRA said four tax filers were convicted over real estate transactions in Vancouver and Toronto.
Canadians flocked to variable-rate mortgages ahead of Bank of Canada rate hikes, CMHC report shows   Share of mortgages with variable interest rates rose above 50%

B.C. Securities Commission alleges broker sold securities without looking out for his clients   Matthew Herbert Calvert was working for North Vancouver-based Ascenta Finance when the securities were sold between July 2016 and August 2019.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

True Crime Byline podcast: This Isn't a Story about Robert Pickton   Listen to this podcast episode for the heartbreaking history of Canada’s most prolific serial killer case, as told by Vancouver Sun reporter Lori Culbert.

Serena Jackson and Caitlin Stockwell: Not in my park? Then, yes, in my neighbourhood   Opinion: When people are sheltering in parks we cannot call for rapid government action to supply housing, and then allow well-planned and funded options to fall through during rezoning

Daphne Bramham: The old West makes way for a new, wilder one   Saskatchewan's Grasslands National Park offers a glimpse of the past (including wild bison roaming), but also a vision for a wilder future

Sonia Furstenau: It’s time to double down on access to reproductive rights and health care in B.C.   Opinion: For people living in northern, rural, and remote areas of B.C., the barriers are massive and unjust

Vaughn Palmer: NDP leadership race provides chance to raise old growth, fracking and LNG issues   Opinion: John Horgan’s pending retirement provides opening to challenge his trade-offs on environmental issues.

Dan Fumano: How much housing does Vancouver need? What kind? And where?   Opinion: With housing set to be a top issue, again, in this year's election, residents will use their votes to show what kind of approach they want to see.
Canucks: Finally time for GM Patrik Allvin and company to shine   This draft presents Allvin and staff with their first real chance to show how they’re going to operate
2022 NHL Draft: What will the Canucks do with their first pick?   The Canucks currently hold the 15th overall pick in the first round of the 2022 NHL Entry Draft.

Former NHLer Bryan Marchment dies suddenly during trip to draft   Marchment played parts of 17 NHL seasons from 1989-2006 with Winnipeg, Chicago, Hartford, Edmonton, Tampa Bay, San Jose, Colorado, Toronto and Calgary.
Canadian swimmer Harvey says she was drugged on last night of championships   Mary-Sophie Harvey said she discovered “dozens of bruises” on her body after travelling home the next day.

Wayne Moriarty: Make no mistake, the breadbasket of young hockey talent is still the CHL   Analysis: At least three Canadian Hockey League players are expected to go in the Top 10 in the 2022 NHL Entry Draft
Indian Summer Festival experiences Inner/Outer Climates for 2022 event   The 2022 Indian Summer Festival celebrates its 12th year.

Vancouver Classical Guitar Festival has a little something for everyone   Luis Angel Medina stresses that the subtext of the Vancouver Classical Guitar Festival is community, a community of players, aficionados, and music lovers. There is lots for players to do, including not just individual lessons but also Medina’s ensemble.

Street dance meets French existentialism in this year's Dancing on the Edge   “The play (Dancing on the Edge) talks about three people who go to Hell and are stuck together for eternity," choreographer Alexandra (Spicey) Landé said.

Culture Shock's American dream is pretty on the outside, but horrifying within   As a voice actor, Mexico City-born Gigi Saul Guerrero has had roles in a number of Netflix animated series. She also teaches at the Vancouver Film School.
Howe Sound spooky setting for Squamish writer's latest psychological thriller   Amber Cowie's Last One Alive offers plenty of perilous action as a group of 10 are picked off one-by-one.

Kasey Wilson: Recreate Southern hospitality with a traditional picnic this summer   Kasey Wilson says it is time to relive the friendliness offered by her family’s large circle of friends in the south

Style Q&A: New Canadian swimwear brand boasts 'affordable and accessible' options for women of all ages   "We take the details and functions found in more 'mature' brands and we adapt them into more contemporary silhouettes."
Anthony Gismondi: B.C. wine for the week of July 7, a bottle to cellar and calendar items   Opinion: The latest B.C. wine country buzz.
Vancouver an 'important market' for Italian luxury brand Gucci   Renovated, expanded boutique reopens at Fairmont Vancouver.
Richmond: A guided tour of an Asian food mecca   A local insider guides a culinary tour of Richmond.
Condo Smarts: Tony Gioventu pens 1,000th column on strata life   Weekly Homes columnist began helping readers with strata-living issues in November, 2002
Two separate plants both called 'sun rose' and 'rock rose'   Both are Mediterranean plants with a preference for similar growing conditions: a very well-drained soil, and full sun.
Sangre de Fruta founder Allison Weldon finds refuge on Bowen Island   Vintage vibes in a modern reno

At home: A Bowen Island refuge with a cool vintage vibe   With her eagle eye for vintage treasures, the founder of Sangre de Fruta Botanical knew a good find when she saw one. In this case, it was an early-’80s specimen: a four-bedroom family home with a West Coast chalet vibe on Bowen Island.
How to make the most of your outdoor areas this summer   But before you dig in, the first step is to have a vision and a clear path forward.

Cancelled Air Canada flights strand Quebec teens at Vancouver airport   A teen scout group heading to the Yukon for a long-planned camping trip rolling ended up rolling out their sleeping bags on the floor of YVR after their Air Canada flight was cancelled
Where are the best places to eat in Kelowna?   Linda Barnard takes a culinary tour to help you decide
Checking In: EXchange Hotel   Boutique hotel cashes in on one-time stock exchange building heritage
Santa Monica will move you   Re-discover SoCal at your own pace
Montréal neighbourhood Saint-Henri a cultural delight   What’s old is new again in trendy and historic area.
'I’m just a driver': Canadian women set course for F1 glory   Could a woman, say, become a member of the most exclusive racing club in the world? Nicole Havrda says that is the plan
Spied! 2023 Ford Super Duty XLT swipes feature from Chevy   The truck was spied in prototype form being put to work in Detroit—but is that a GM-style integrated step?
Mercedes strikes second-life battery pact with Canadian startup   It will supply used batteries from the automaker’s EVs to Vancouver-based Moment Energy Inc.
Watch: 15-year-old flips Jeep off cliff, reacts like a boss   Okay, but our hearts were beating pretty fast before that “Somebody better have gotten that on video!”
GTA Lamborghini driver nabbed at nearly 3X the speed limit   “..we never get tired of yanking stunt drivers off our roads.”
TikTok Tuesday: #healthycoke — a trend that’s not so healthy for your teeth (WATCH)   It may be low in sugar, but carbonated water mixed with vinegar is a terrible choice for your teeth.
Putting our phones on silent mode only makes them more tempting, study says   Blame FOMO for how hard it can be for us to stop looking at our phones all the time.

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Man's ploy to flush over 20 kilograms of cannabis down the toilet leads him straight to jail   Officers also found 21 kilograms of weed, additional bags of prepackaged marijuana, a handgun and over $16K in cash.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
One of Canada's biggest universities will have a pot store on campus   The planned store would represent the pot company Burb's eighth and final one allowed in the province under its retail cap.
Ontario casinos laundered $3 million in illegal cannabis proceeds: RCMP   Weed worth approx. $24.8 million confiscated.
Clint Eastwood handed $2.6 million after winning a second trademark infringement CBD lawsuit   Company did not have permission to use his image or likeness.
